message:
feat(wasm): extend wasm bindings with additional exported functions and update type declarations

Expand fm-wasm with new exported functions and update TypeScript
type declarations to reflect the extended API surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -340,9 +340,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.3","title":"Fix npm package name: README says @frankenmermaid/core but pkg is fm-wasm","description":"Reality check found: README says 'npm install @frankenmermaid/core' and all TypeScript import examples use '@frankenmermaid/core', but pkg/package.json has name 'fm-wasm'.

## Decision
Rename the package to '@frankenmermaid/core' to match the README branding. This is the user-facing name and must be consistent.

## Scope
1. Update pkg/package.json: change name from 'fm-wasm' to '@frankenmermaid/core'
2. Update build-wasm.sh if it sets or references the package name
3. Check if wasm-pack generates the name field — if so, update Cargo.toml [package] metadata or the wasm-pack --out-name flag
4. Update any internal imports that reference 'fm-wasm' as a package name
5. Verify the package.json has correct metadata: description, repository, homepage, keywords, license fields
6. Ensure the package has a proper README in the npm package (copy or symlink main README)

## Verification
- cat pkg/package.json | jq .name should output '@frankenmermaid/core'
- re-run build-wasm.sh and verify the generated package.json still has the correct name
- The TypeScript definition file imports should work with the new name

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.6 (TypeScript defs) depends on this — do this first
- Related to bd-6qd.2 (NPM package distribution, already closed) — this is a naming fix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:55.816766474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:41:14.239368480Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:41:14.239089281Z","close_reason":"Fixed the WASM npm package branding to @frankenmermaid/core, made build-wasm.sh regenerate package metadata and pkg/README.md deterministically, and repaired wasm32 build blockers; remaining gzip-budget failure is tracked separately in bd-kugr.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","npm","reality-check","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:37:55.816766474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.4","title":"Verify and fix install.sh curl installer end-to-end","description":"Reality check found: README prominently features 'curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash' as the primary install method, but this has never been verified end-to-end.

## Scope
1. Read install.sh and document what it does (download pre-built binary? build from source? check platform?)
2. Test in a clean environment: run the install script and verify fm-cli is available afterward
3. Fix any issues found: broken URLs, missing platform detection, incorrect binary names
4. Verify the installed binary passes a smoke test: 'echo flowchart LR; A-->B | fm-cli detect -' should output 'Flowchart'
5. Add a CI job (GitHub Actions) that runs the installer in a clean ubuntu container and verifies the binary works
6. If the installer downloads from GitHub Releases, verify releases exist and contain the correct binaries

## Test Plan
- Run install.sh in a docker ubuntu:latest container
- Verify fm-cli is in PATH after install
- Run: fm-cli detect, fm-cli render --format svg, fm-cli capabilities
- Verify non-zero exit code on genuinely bad input
- Log all output with timestamps for debugging

## Cross-reference
- Related to bd-6qd.3 (CLI binary distribution via GitHub Releases)
- Related to bd-6qd.5 (release canary workflow) — the installer test IS a canary","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.117219987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:53:05.281108873Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["distribution","install","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.117219987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.5","title":"Verify cargo install frankenmermaid path or update README","description":"Reality check found: README says 'cargo install frankenmermaid' under Installation but the crate is not published to crates.io.

## Scope
1. Check if 'frankenmermaid' crate exists on crates.io (curl https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/frankenmermaid)
2. If NOT published:
   a. Option A (preferred if close to release): Prepare Cargo.toml metadata for publishing — all workspace crates need: description, license, repository, categories, keywords, readme
   b. Option B (if not ready): Update README to remove 'cargo install frankenmermaid' section and only show 'From Source' install method, with a note that crates.io publishing is planned
3. If published but outdated: update or add note about version
4. Verify the binary name would be 'fm-cli' (check [[bin]] section in workspace Cargo.toml)
5. Verify 'cargo install frankenmermaid' actually installs the binary correctly (if published)

## Decision Criteria
- If more than 3 workspace crates have missing metadata: go with Option B (update README) now, create separate bead for crates.io publishing prep
- If metadata is mostly complete: go with Option A

## Verification
- After fix: README installation section only references working install methods
- No broken commands in the Installation section

## Cross-reference
- Related to bd-6qd.3 (CLI binary distribution) and bd-6qd.5 (release canary)
- This is about the README claim, not the full publishing workflow","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.300696615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:33:38.074959434Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:33:38.074665588Z","close_reason":"Completed: crates.io API for frankenmermaid returns 404, fm-cli is the actual binary, and more than three workspace crates are missing publish metadata; README installation now advertises only working install paths and notes crates.io publishing is planned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","distribution","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.5","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.300696615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-b0pc.6","title":"Regenerate TypeScript definitions to include missing WASM exports","description":"Reality check found: pkg/frankenmermaid.d.ts is missing TypeScript definitions for 3 exported WASM functions: diagramLens, applyLensEdit, describeDiagram. These ARE implemented in Rust and exported via wasm_bindgen, but the .d.ts was generated before they were added.

## Scope
1. Re-run wasm-pack build (via build-wasm.sh) to regenerate .d.ts
2. Verify all 10 functions appear in the new .d.ts: init, renderSvg, detectType, parse, sourceSpans, diagramLens, applyLensEdit, describeDiagram, capabilityMatrix, render
3. Verify the Diagram class and all its methods appear: constructor, render, setTheme, on, destroy
4. Verify function signatures have correct parameter types (not just 'any' for everything)
5. Commit the updated .d.ts, .js glue, and .wasm binary
6. If build-wasm.sh fails, fix the build script (may need wasm-pack/wasm-opt installed)

## Test Plan
- diff old .d.ts vs new .d.ts — should show 3+ new export lines
- TypeScript compiler check: write a small .ts file that imports all 10 functions and the Diagram class — verify it compiles with tsc --noEmit
- Count exports: grep -c 'export function\|export class' pkg/frankenmermaid.d.ts should be >= 11

## Cross-reference
- Depends on bd-b0pc.3 (npm package name fix) — want correct name in generated defs
- Requires wasm-pack toolchain (may need to be installed on build machine)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.486380153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:20:54.171485606Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-check","typescript","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:13.486380153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:39:33.053329066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.7","title":"Verify and wire up frankenmermaid.toml config file loading in CLI","description":"CRITICAL GAP: README documents '--config <path>' as a Global Flag (line 253), but the actual clap Cli struct in main.rs has NO --config argument. The config file feature is entirely unimplemented in the CLI. The README also shows a detailed frankenmermaid.toml format with [core], [parser], [layout], [render], [svg], [term] sections.

## Scope (implementation required, not just verification)
1. Add --config <path> global argument to the Cli clap struct in main.rs
2. Add config file auto-discovery: check ./frankenmermaid.toml, then ~/.config/frankenmermaid/config.toml
3. Define a FrankenmermaidConfig struct matching the documented TOML schema:
   - [core]: deterministic, max_input_bytes, fallback_on_error
   - [parser]: intent_inference, fuzzy_keyword_distance, auto_close_delimiters, create_placeholder_nodes
   - [layout]: algorithm, cycle_strategy, node_spacing, rank_spacing, edge_routing
   - [render]: default_format, show_back_edges, reduced_motion
   - [svg]: theme, rounded_corners, shadows, gradients, accessibility
   - [term]: tier, unicode, minimap
4. Parse TOML config file using toml crate (add as dependency if not present)
5. Merge config into existing SvgRenderConfig, TermRenderConfig, LayoutConfig structs
6. CLI flags override config file values (flags take precedence)
7. Add clear error messages for: file not found, invalid TOML, unknown keys
8. Log which config file was loaded (at -v verbosity level)

## Test Plan
- Unit test: parse sample TOML into FrankenmermaidConfig struct
- Unit test: merge config with CLI defaults (config overrides defaults, CLI flags override config)
- Integration test: write temp TOML with theme=dark, run fm-cli render --config <file>, verify dark SVG
- Negative test: --config with non-existent file produces clear error with path
- Negative test: malformed TOML produces error with line number
- Discovery test: create frankenmermaid.toml in temp dir, run fm-cli from that dir, verify auto-loaded
- Precedence test: config says theme=dark, CLI says --theme forest, verify forest wins

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.12 depends on this (config round-trip tests)
- Related to bd-6qd.4 (versioned config schema) — this is runtime loading, that's schema formalization
- toml crate may need to be added to fm-cli Cargo.toml dependencies","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.477899944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:00.984817349Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","config","reality-check"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.477899944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-b0pc.8","title":"Improve sequence diagram fragment rendering (loop/alt/par nesting)","description":"Reality check found: Sequence diagrams are marked 'Partial' in the README. The parser comprehensively captures fragments (loop, alt, par, opt, neg), boxes, notes, activations, and 26 arrow types. But layout/rendering of fragment nesting may be incomplete.

## Scope
1. Create comprehensive test inputs with nested fragments:
   a. Simple fragment: 'loop Every minute; A->>B: ping; end'
   b. Nested alt/else: 'alt success; A->>B: ok; else failure; A->>B: err; end'
   c. Double-nested: 'loop; alt; A->>B: yes; else; A->>B: no; end; end'
   d. Par blocks: 'par; A->>B: one; and; A->>C: two; end'
   e. Fragment with box: 'box Group; participant A; participant B; end'
2. Render each to SVG and terminal, inspect output:
   a. Are fragment boundaries visually rendered (labeled rectangles)?
   b. Are nested fragments properly indented/contained?
   c. Do fragment labels (loop, alt, else, par) appear in the output?
   d. Are activation bars rendered on lifelines?
3. If fragments are NOT visually rendered:
   a. Add fragment boundary rendering to fm-render-svg: draw labeled rounded-rect for each fragment region
   b. Add fragment support to fm-render-term: draw box chars around fragment regions
   c. The LayoutExtensions.sequence_fragments (or similar) field should carry fragment geometry
4. If fragments ARE rendered but poorly:
   a. Fix nesting overlap issues
   b. Ensure label positioning doesn't collide with messages
5. Once complete: update README status from 'Partial' to 'Implemented'

## Test Plan
- Golden SVG test for each of the 5 fragment test cases above
- Assert SVG output contains 'rect' elements for fragment boundaries (not just messages)
- Assert terminal output has box-drawing characters around fragment regions
- Property test: any valid sequence input with fragments produces SVG with fragment-count rect elements

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.11 (e2e smoke tests) depends on this
- No existing bead covers sequence fragment rendering specifically","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:06:24.783241358Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","reality-check","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.8","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b0pc.9","title":"Add PNG rasterization smoke test under --features png","description":"Reality check found: README documents PNG output via '--features png' using resvg/usvg, but there are zero tests for this feature in the CI suite.

## Scope
1. Verify the png feature compiles: cargo check -p fm-cli --features png
2. If it fails: fix Cargo.toml dependency versions for resvg/usvg, update any API changes
3. Create integration test in fm-cli/tests/ (feature-gated with #[cfg(feature = "png")]):
   a. Render 'flowchart LR; A-->B-->C' to PNG via the render pipeline
   b. Verify output file exists and is non-empty
   c. Check PNG magic bytes: first 8 bytes should be [0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A]
   d. Verify image dimensions are reasonable (> 100x100 pixels, < 10000x10000)
   e. Test with different themes: default and dark should produce different PNG content
4. Add CI job or test configuration that runs with --features png
5. If resvg/usvg are too heavy for CI, add a note about optional PNG support

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p fm-cli --features png -- png (run only PNG-specific tests)
- Assert PNG magic bytes
- Assert file size > 1KB (not empty/trivial)
- Assert multiple diagram types produce valid PNGs (flowchart + sequence at minimum)
- Log file sizes and generation times for performance baseline

## Cross-reference
- PNG feature is documented in README under 'fm-cli render' and under 'Feature Flags'
- No existing bead covers PNG testing","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:06:39.696179011Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["png","reality-check","render","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.9","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.129238031Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.128753604Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bds1","title":"Sequence parity: dotted-cross distinction and central-offset nuance","description":"Follow-up parity bead for remaining sequence message semantics after half-arrow and central connection support.
